definition: an expression used when parting from someone
hook: Every time you say goodbye, you're saying a prayer.
modernAnchor: You wave it at the door. You text it ten times a day.

origin.originalMeaning: a prayer for divine protection on the parted

journey: It started as a full prayer: 'God be with ye,' spoken when one person left another.
Travel was dangerous and partings might be final, so you left them in God's hands.
Over centuries it contracted: godbwye, then godbye, until it became goodbye.
The 'good' you say isn't really good at all. It's a worn-down version of God.
